---
title: "Preparacion de review"
author: "Francisco D. Londoño-García"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# cls: apa.cls \Indica que se utilice formato de citación APA.
# bibliography: bibliografia_review_MEng.bib \Tomar referencias del archivo indicado

# Librerías útiles

```

# Análisis de artículos

A partir de una revisión bibliográfica en [*Web of Science*](https://clarivate.com/academia-government/scientific-and-academic-research/research-discovery-and-referencing/web-of-science/) utilizando las palabras clave:

- Hop oil, Extracts.
- Hop oil, Solvent extraction.
- Hop oil, Microwave extraction.

Se introdujeron los artículos proporcionados en la web [*Tree of Science*](https://tos.coreofscience.com) para la identificación de características y referencias en común y realizar una revisión bibliográfica más concreta y con menos redundancias. La información proporcionada arrojó la categorización de aproximadamente cuarenta y cinco (45) referencias bibliográficas.

## Raíces

Los artículos identificados en esta etapa son los puntos de origen para los otros trabajos analizados.

### Elementos de interés

#### Hop Compounds: Extraction techniques, chemical analyses, antioxidative, antimicrobial, and anticarcinogenic effects

This contribution provides an overview of extraction and fractionation techniques of the most important hop compounds known for their health-promoting features.

Numerous publications on isolation of active compounds have appeared over the past two decades regarding a wide range of natural flavor formulations that are readily available including essential oils, herb and spice extracts, flavor substances fractionated from essential oils, or botanical extracts and natural flavor substances produced by fermentation.

Technologies used to isolate compounds with certain biological activity and to produce natural flavor formulations are well established, but several involve the use of conventional solvents like alcohols (methanol and ethanol), acetone, diethyl ether, or ethyl acetate and the use of methods that are energy intensive. Solvents that are produced from fossil fuels need to be tightly controlled with respect to their residual levels. The residues of the applied solvents may also remain in the final products bearing a detrimental effect on human health. This requires additional purification steps that are time-consuming and increase the total process cost. In applying the ideologies of green chemistry, water and carbon dioxide clearly represent safer solvents and the use of a renewable feedstock possesses the widest acceptability.

Several modifications of these processes have been reported with respect to the extraction procedure, the choice of an appropriate solvent and cosolvent for the isolation of the desired compound and the tunability of operating conditions. Supercritical $CO_{2}$ still remains the solvent of choice for these operations and indeed more than 90 % of supercritical fluid extractions have been performed with $CO_{2}$ as the supercritical solvent, mainly because of its easy penetration inside plant materials and high solvent power. Disadvantageously, its use is largely limited to the processinf of dry raw materials and compounds of low polarity as well as low molecular weight.

**VOY PARA LA SECCIÓN 2. METHODS OF ISOLATION, CHARACTERIZATION, AND DETERMINATION OF ANTIOXIDATIVE, ANTIMICROBIAL, AND ANTIGENOTOXIC POTENTIALS OF MAIN CHEMICAL COMPOUNDS IN HOPS**

## Tronco

## Ramas

### Rama 1

### Rama 2

### Rama 3

## Hojas
